QuestionText:
Which of the following describes encapsulation?
(A) Hiding the internal state...
(B) A class inheriting...
(C) Creating multiple methods...
In Java a class can implement multiple interfaces.\nPlease enter true or false

Which keyword is used to call a parent class constructor in Java?
(A) this
(B) parent
(C) super
Polymorphism allows a subclass to have a different implementation of a method.\nPlease enter true or false

What is an abstract class?
(A) A class that cannot be instantiated.
(B) A class with no methods.
(C) A class that cannot be inherited.
Method overloading and method overriding are exactly the same concept.\nPlease enter true or false

Which of these access modifiers restricts access the most?
(A) protected
(B) private
(C) public
In OOP an interface can contain concrete method implementations in Java 8 and above.\nPlease enter true or false

Which principle states that objects of a superclass should be replaceable with objects of its subclasses?
(A) Open-Closed Principle
(B) Liskov Substitution Principle
(C) Interface Segregation Principle
A final class in Java can be extended by other classes.\nPlease enter true or false

What is the derivative of x^2?
(A) 2x
(B) x
(C) 2
The square root of 169 is 13. Please enter true or false

Solve for x: 3x - 9 = 0
(A) 2
(B) 3
(C) 4
(D) 5
Pi is a rational number.\nPlease enter true or false

What is the integral of 2x dx?
(A) x^2 + C
(B) 2x^2 + C
(C) x + C
The sum of the interior angles of a triangle is always 180 degrees.\nPlease enter true or false

If a = 3 and b = 4 what is the length of the hypotenuse?
(A) 5
(B) 6
(C) 7
The determinant of an identity matrix is always 0.\nPlease enter true or false

What is the value of 5 factorial (5!)?
(A) 60
(B) 100
(C) 120
A prime number has exactly two distinct positive divisors: 1 and itself.\nPlease enter true or false
If all men are mortal, and Socrates is a man, then Socrates is mortal.\nPlease enter true or false

Which logical connective represents 'AND'?
(A) Conjunction
(B) Disjunction
(C) Implication
The statement 'A and not A' can be true at the same time.\nPlease enter true or false

If P implies Q, and P is true, what can we deduce about Q?
(A) Q is false
(B) Q is true
(C) Q is unknown
Modus Tollens states that if P implies Q, and Q is false, then P must be false.\nPlease enter true or false

In Boolean algebra, what is the result of '1 OR 0'?
(A) 0
(B) 1
(C) Null
De Morgan's laws state that NOT (P AND Q) is equivalent to (NOT P) OR (NOT Q).\nPlease enter true or false

Which of the following represents a tautology?
(A) P OR (NOT P)
(B) P AND (NOT P)
(C) P implies (NOT P)
A valid deductive argument can have true premises and a false conclusion.\nPlease enter true or false

If 'Some A are B' is true, which of the following must also be true?
(A) All A are B
(B) Some B are A
(C) No A are B
